what_it_does: This file stores YAML-frontmatter metadata for a channel attachment that links an image asset in the design-assets channel.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
It declares an attachment id att-0b8dbe36, type images, and channel design-assets.
The target field uses a URI-like scheme image://design-assets/icons/image (33).
An ISO-8601 added timestamp is present.
standouts: The target URI contains spaces and parentheses: 'image (33)'.
The attachment record is purely metadata with no descriptive title or alt text.
The filename follows an att-<uuid>.md pattern.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Follows a channel-memory attachment naming convention att-<uuid>.md.
References assets with an image:// scheme.
concerns: The target string contains unescaped spaces and parentheses, which may cause parsing or URL issues.
The image filename 'image (33)' is generic and gives no semantic hint about the asset.
There is no recorded author, MIME type, dimensions, or alt text.
notes: A minimal, timestamped metadata record that points a design-assets channel attachment to a specific image URI.
questions: What actual image file does image://design-assets/icons/image (33) resolve to?
Who or what process added this attachment?
What system consumes these att-<uuid>.md records?
Is the space/parenthesis in the target URI intentional and supported by the resolver?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — no explicit stated purpose in the file; the fields are consistent with an image attachment record.
intention_signals: It appears intended to register a specific image asset as an attachment in the design-assets channel so it can be referenced, retrieved, or rendered later.
novelty: The target URI includes literal spaces and parentheses and a generic 'image (33)' name, making the record unusually opaque for an asset pointer.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, typed pointer from a channel attachment identifier to a specific image asset location, preserving provenance and a retrievable reference.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record in company/channel-memory that registers an images-type attachment att-0b8dbe36 in the design-assets channel, pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (33) and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to catalog and make retrievable a specific design image asset within the design-assets channel.
